META.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Earnings fired TODAY (April 29, 2026) — this is a just-occurred catalyst. The primary trade thesis is the post-earnings reaction. Daily close was ~$669 but hourly current price has crashed to ~$603, representing an approximately 10% drop from the pre-earnings level. This is a material negative earnings reaction based on the hourly data showing price -10% below all hourly moving averages, RSI 21 at 14.7 (deeply oversold), and hourly relative volume at 9.99x normal — all hallmarks of a large institutional sell-off following an earnings disappointment or guidance miss. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Mixed-to-negative post-event. Going into earnings, the catalyst stack was constructive (Muse Spark AI launch Apr 8, $21B CoreWeave deal, eMarketer projecting META to surpass Google in global digital ad revenue). However, the magnitude of the post-earnings price drop suggests the actual Q1 report or Q2 guidance failed to meet elevated expectations. The social media addiction litigation overhang (California 70% liability verdict, 10,000+ lawsuits, New Mexico $375M award) likely weighed on sentiment. Net news flow is negative post-catalyst. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Net selling — wiki notes 668 insider sells vs. 240 buys in the prior period; no large insider buys flagged. This was explicitly characterized as a momentum/catalyst trade, not an insider-conviction trade. Bearish lean from insider data. TECHNICAL SETUP: Daily setup was constructive going into earnings (ADX 49 strong trend, above all MAs, RSI 14 at 65.6, MACD positive). However, the post-earnings hourly reaction has shattered that setup: price is now -9.5% to -10% below all hourly EMAs/SMAs, hourly ADX shows minus_di at 55.14 vs plus_di at 8.27 (strong downtrend momentum on hourly), and Bollinger pct_b at -0.993 (well below lower band). The daily support at $627.61 (11 tests) is the first meaningful floor; the Fib 38.2% retracement at $626.10 aligns closely. FORWARD-LOOKING: With no new catalyst within the next 10 trading days (FOMC was same day, Muse Spark rollout is ongoing but not a binary event), the stock likely enters a period of post-earnings multiple compression. The 2-5 day window should see continued selling pressure as institutions re-rate guidance. Bullish counterargument: analyst consensus remains 61 Buy / 6 Hold with $860 consensus target; hourly RSI at 14.7 is extremely oversold and a technical bounce is possible intraday. But the trend of the catalyst reaction is decisively negative, and stops should be placed above pre-earnings levels.
